data.title: FCC raises concerns over Netflix-Warner Bros. merger
data.description:
Federal Communications Commission chairman Brendan Carr has expressed concerns over a potential merger of Netflix (NFLX) and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD).

“There are legitimate competition concerns that I’ve seen raised about [a Netflix] acquisition and just the sheer amount of scale and consolidation you can see in the streaming market,” Carr said in an interview with Bloomberg.

Although Carr views a Netflix acquisition of Warner Bros.’ (WBD) film and TV studio assets as being problematic, he has fewer reservations over a potential takeover of the entire business by Paramount Skydance (PSKY) given the comparatively smaller size of the Paramount+ streaming service.

While the FCC has no jurisdiction over the potential merger between Netflix (NFLX) and Warner Bros. (WBD), a Paramount Skydance/Warner Bros. merger would fall under the FCC’s purview due to Paramount’s (PSKY) intention to raise funds from foreign sources to finance the transaction.

data.title: Coniagas appoints Heidi Gutte as CFO
data.description:
• Coniagas Battery Metals (COS:CA) on Friday announced the appointment of Heidi Gutte as Chief Financial Officer, effective January 23, 2026.
  • Gutte is replacing Remantra Sheopaul. Sheopaul resigned from the CFO position effective January 21, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: XTL announces receipt of Nasdaq notification
data.description:
• XTL Biopharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:XTLB) said on Friday it received a notice from Nasdaq on January 20, 2026, that the company is not in compliance with the continued listing requirements to maintain the minimum $2.5M in stockholders’ equity.
  • Nasdaq said its determination was based on the company’s Dec. 30, 2025, Form 6-K showing a $47,000 stockholders’ equity deficit and its failure to meet alternative listing standards as of Jan. 20, 2026.
  • The company said it is evaluating options to regain compliance and intends to do so within Nasdaq’s required timeframe.

data.title: RJK Explorations announces private placement of up to $130,000
data.description:
• RJK Explorations (RJX.A:CA) on Friday said that it plans a non-brokered private placement to raise gross proceeds of up to $130,000.

  • The company said the offering will consist of units priced at $0.025 each, with each unit comprising one common share and one warrant.

  • Each warrant allows the holder to purchase one additional common share at $0.05 for a period of five years from issuance.

  • RJK said it intends to use about 80% of the proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes, with the remainder allocated to fund its exploration program.
  • The offering is subject to customary closing conditions, including approval from the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • RJKAF closed +310.0% at $0.0041.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SLB ready to quickly ramp up Venezuela activity, CEO says
data.description:
SLB (SLB) CEO Olivier Le Peuch said Friday the company is well-positioned to quickly expand its business in Venezuela, given its role as the only international oilfield services provider that has maintained an active operating presence in the country, delivering services for Chevron under the oil producer's license.

"With appropriate licensing, safety parameters and compliance measures in place, we can rapidly ramp up activities," Le Peuch said on the company's earnings conference call. "We're already receiving a lot of incoming calls to explore options."

"We have the track record" in Venezuela and a significant set of assets ready to be deployed, the CEO said, noting SLB (SLB) a decade ago employed more than 3K workers in the country and recorded more than $1B in annual revenues.

Rival oilfield services firm Halliburton said earlier this week that it aims to quickly grow its business in Venezuela as soon as it secures U.S. government approval and certainty over payments.

Stifel analyst Stephen Gengaro has said SLB (SLB) and Halliburton are among the best-positioned companies to benefit from any new investment that flows into Venezuela.

SLB (SLB) shares closed -0.3% on Friday after the company beat Wall Street estimates for Q4 adjusted earnings while revenues rose 5% to $9.75B, also above expectations, and net income fell to $824M, or $0.55/share, from $1.1B, or $0.77/share, in the year-earlier quarter.

The company said it expects a decline in adjusted EBITDA and revenues during the current quarter, in part due to outsized product sales at the end of last year, but revenues are seen rising for the full year, guiding for $36.9B-$37.7B in revenues, up from $35.7B in FY 2025, helped by the ChampionX acquisition, strong international activity, and faster than expected growth across its data center solutions business.

data.title: Digital Currency X Technology receives Nasdaq delisting notice
data.description:
• Digital Currency X Technology (DCX) on Friday said that it received a notice from Nasdaq stating that it is not in compliance with the exchange’s $1.00 minimum bid price requirement.
  • The company said its Class A ordinary shares closed below $1.00 for 30 consecutive business days between December 4, 2025 and January 16, 2026.
  • The exchange said that the company is not eligible for a compliance period under its listing rules because it has effected a reverse stock split within the past year or multiple reverse stock splits over the past two years meeting certain thresholds.
  • Nasdaq said the company’s securities will be delisted from the Nasdaq Capital Market unless Digital Currency X requests an appeal by January 27, 2026. If no appeal is requested, trading would be suspended at the open on January 29, 2026.
  • Digital Currency X said it plans to request a hearing to appeal the determination.
  • The company previously completed a 12-for-1 share consolidation effective January 22, 2026, but said there is no assurance it will regain or maintain compliance with Nasdaq listing standards.
  • DCX closed -3.91% at $4.785.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: USCB targets $100M association banking deposit growth while expanding lending in key Florida markets
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: USCB Financial Holdings (USCB) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Luis de la Aguilera reported that 2025 delivered strong results for USCB, highlighting "total assets reached $2.8 billion, up 8.1% year-over-year. Loans grew by $216 million or 11%, reflecting strong commercial activity and disciplined underwriting. Deposits increased $171 million or 7.9%, demonstrating continued franchise growth and deep client relationships." He noted that net interest income expanded to 3.27% and tangible book value per share rose 10.8% to $11.97, underscoring business model strength across profitability, balance sheet, credit quality, and capital.

  • Management completed a $40 million subordinated debt issuance in Q3 2025 and used most proceeds to repurchase about 2 million shares at $17.19 per share. Aguilera explained this "underscoring our confidence in the intrinsic value of our stock and our commitment to returning capital to shareholders."

  • For Q4, USCB reported GAAP diluted EPS of $0.07, which included a $0.31 per share after-tax loss from a $44.6 million securities sale and a $0.06 per share income tax liability from prior periods. Excluding these items, operational diluted EPS was $0.44, which Aguilera described as "consistent with last quarter and reflecting strong stable performance." He emphasized Q4 was the "strongest loan production quarter for the year, and this past December, posted a record monthly closing high for 2025."

  • CFO Robert Anderson stated, "Adjusting our GAAP figures for these 2 items only, you will find the operating or adjusted numbers on Page 6. This includes operating return on average assets of 1.14%, operating return on average equity of 15.05%, operating efficiency of 55.92% and operating diluted earnings per share of $0.44." Anderson discussed balance sheet resilience and expected further NIM improvement in 2026, supported by deposit repricing and balance sheet repositioning.

  • Chief Credit Officer William Turner reported, "The allowance for credit losses increased to $25.5 million at the end of the fourth quarter at an adequate 1.16% of the portfolio... There were no loan losses during the quarter." Turner highlighted nonperforming loans at 0.14% of total loans, driven by two past due residential real estate loans that are "well collateralized by real estate and no loss is expected."

Outlook

  • Management outlined a focus on expanding and strengthening the deposit base for 2026, with four primary business lines: Business Banking, Private Client Group, Association Banking, and Correspondent Banking. Aguilera stated, "Our 2026 strategy focus on property management company. Roughly half of the HOAs in South Florida are professionally managed. And now with 25 firms onboarded, we are working hard to capture these operating and reserve balances."

  • Business banking is launching a new lending and deposit gathering team for Doral, Medley, and Hialeah, targeting SBA and C&I lending, operating accounts, and treasury services. The Private Client Group aims to add more relationship talent in early 2026. Association Banking targets $100 million in deposit growth. Correspondent Banking seeks to onboard 3 to 5 new banks and expand low-cost deposits.

  • CFO Anderson indicated tax expense should be modeled at 26.4% for 2026.

Financial Results

  • USCB reported Q4 GAAP diluted EPS of $0.07, with operational diluted EPS at $0.44 after adjusting for securities restructuring and tax items. Net interest income increased $933,000 on a linked quarter basis and by $2.8 million year-over-year. NIM expanded 13 basis points quarter-over-quarter and 11 basis points year-over-year to 3.27%.

  • Average loans increased $31.9 million compared to the prior quarter, and $172.3 million year-over-year. Gross loan production totaled $196 million, with $83.5 million from correspondent banking. Portfolio yield declined to 6.16% due to a higher proportion of short-tenure SOFR-linked loans.

  • Noninterest income, excluding the securities loss, was $3.3 million. The expense base for the quarter was $14.3 million, including a $759,000 bonus plan expense and $275,000 in nonroutine legal and consulting fees. Adjusted efficiency ratio, after removing these items, would have been 51.87%.

  • The Board approved a 25% increase in the quarterly cash dividend to $0.125 per share.

Q&A

  • William Jones, KBW: Asked about deposit shrinkage at year-end and seasonality. CEO Aguilera responded, "We have a relationship with a client that is over 10 years... there was a significant move in deposits of well over $100 million... The client still maintains with us over $112 million in deposits... there was also on the correspondent banking side, about a $50 million swing... that has pretty much been recovered already in January."

  • Jones followed up on the loan-to-deposit ratio. CFO Anderson replied, "I like kind of the 90% to 95% on the loan-to-deposit ratio... we anticipate to build back those deposits."

  • Jones inquired about the SBA vertical expansion. Aguilera explained, "The initiative actually launched about 4 years ago... delivered over $1 million in fee income last year... We'd like to grow this probably in the next 3 years to about $40 million or $50 million in annual volume."

  • Feddie Strickland, Hovde Group: Asked about margin outlook. CFO Anderson replied, "I think you should model flat to slightly up, not significant, and we look to build it."

  • Strickland questioned loan growth expectations. Anderson said, "I would still say conservatively, certainly high single digits and maybe a little bit more to the low double digits would be kind of a secondary guide for you."

  • Evan Yee, Raymond James: Asked about expense outlook. Anderson responded, "Our expense base was around $13.2 million for the quarter. And I think you'll see that gradually increase in the first quarter with new hires and through the year."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed neutral to slightly positive sentiment, probing for clarity on deposit outflows, loan growth, and expense outlook, with questions focused on sustainability and growth prospects.

  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing operational stability, growth initiatives, and conservative risk management. Aguilera expressed confidence: "We continue to proactively make ongoing strategic decisions that support profitability and shareholder value."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistently confident, with analysts continuing to seek detail on deposit trends and growth verticals but without notable skepticism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize targeted deposit growth in Association Banking and new lending teams, compared to Q3's broader focus on scalable deposit-focused verticals and loan growth.

  • Strategic focus expanded from maintaining strong operational results to targeted market expansion in select Florida markets and scaling specialty business lines for deposit growth.

  • Analysts in both quarters concentrated on margin, expenses, and loan/deposit trends, but Q4 saw more attention to nonrecurring items impacting GAAP results and forward-looking efficiency.

  • Key metrics such as NIM, loan growth, and efficiency ratio remained robust, though Q4 reported notable nonrecurring losses impacting GAAP EPS, which were absent in Q3.

  • Management's confidence in growth and resilience persisted, with greater specificity in plans for business vertical expansion and talent investment in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged isolated deposit outflows in late Q4 due to a long-standing client and correspondent bank activity, both characterized as expected and largely recovered.

  • The securities portfolio restructuring led to a notable loss, but proceeds were reinvested in higher-yielding loans to support future earnings.

  • Expense increases included new bonus and retention programs and one-time legal/consulting fees, with management indicating these are investments in future growth and talent retention.

  • Credit quality risks remain low, with increases in nonperforming and classified loans attributed to two residential real estate loans that are well collateralized and not expected to result in losses.

Final Takeaway

USCB Financial Holdings closed 2025 with strong operating performance, highlighted by stable core earnings, record loan production, and targeted expansion of deposit-gathering initiatives. Strategic actions, including securities portfolio restructuring and investments in business verticals, are positioned to enhance earnings power in 2026. Management remains focused on disciplined growth in key Florida markets, strengthening core funding, and sustaining top-tier credit quality, supporting long-term value creation for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SouthState signals 4% expense growth and mid- to upper single-digit loan expansion for 2026 as integration drives momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: SouthState Bank Corporation (SSB) Q4 2025

Management View

  • John Corbett, CEO & Director, highlighted the successful completion and integration of the Independent Financial acquisition: "During this past year of 2025, the SouthState team successfully navigated through that initial period of high risks, the regulatory approvals and the systems conversions, and now we're on the other side, enjoying the rewards of a well-choreographed integration." Corbett also recognized Mark Thompson for leading the integration and announced his upcoming retirement.
  • Corbett stated that, excluding merger costs, "earnings per share in 2025 are up over 30%... double-digit growth in tangible book value per share... raising the dividend by 11% and share repurchases." He noted 8% loan and deposit growth during the quarter.
  • The company executed a share repurchase of 2 million shares in Q4 and the Board authorized a new plan for an additional 5.56 million shares. Corbett emphasized, "We didn't want to miss the opportunity to retire shares when there was such a disconnect between the fundamental performance of the bank and the valuation."
  • CFO William Matthews reported, "We had a good quarter to close out a very good year with PPNR of $323 million and $2.47 in EPS, resulting in a full year PPNR of $1.27 billion and EPS of $9.50. Our return on tangible common equity for the year was approximately 20%." Matthews also noted the efficiency ratio remained below 50% for both the quarter and the year, and capital ratios ended strong with a TCE ratio of 8.8% and CET1 at 11.4%.

Outlook

  • Margin guidance remains unchanged, with Stephen Young, Chief Strategy Officer, stating, "We would expect NIM to continue to be between 3.80% to 3.90% in 2026." Interest-earning assets are expected to average $61 billion to $62 billion, and loan accretion is forecasted at $125 million for 2026.
  • Corbett reiterated previous guidance for mid- to upper single-digit loan growth in 2026, supported by a $5 billion pipeline. He noted, "Our guidance previously for 2026 was mid- to upper single-digit loan growth. We still think that, that is appropriate as we see these pipelines build and hold."
  • Noninterest expense is projected to increase by 4% over 2025's $1.407 billion, reflecting investments in revenue producers and inflationary pressures, according to Matthews.

Financial Results

  • Matthews detailed, "Net interest income of $581 million, which was down $19 million from Q3 or up $14 million, excluding accretion." Loan and deposit growth both reached 8% annualized in Q4. Noninterest income rose to $106 million, driven by the Capital Markets division's $31 million revenue.
  • Cost of deposits and total cost of funds both declined, with deposit costs down 9 basis points and total cost of funds down 14 basis points. Loan yields of 6.13% were down 35 basis points, aligning with new loan origination coupons of 6.06%.
  • The company repurchased 2 million shares at an average price of $90.65. Total payout ratio was just shy of 100% for the quarter. Tangible book value per share grew 10% year-over-year.

Q&A

  • John McDonald, Truist Securities, asked about net interest margin assumptions; Young confirmed that guidance and deposit beta expectations are unchanged from last quarter. Corbett discussed loan growth momentum, citing a 16% quarter-over-quarter increase in production and a stable $5 billion pipeline.
  • Stephen Scouten, Piper Sandler, inquired about hiring activity; Corbett stated the bank had added 550 to 600 commercial RMs and may increase that by 10% to 15% in the next year or two. Scouten also asked about the sustainability of correspondent banking performance; Young explained that while recent performance benefited from rate changes, the business is expected to average $25 million per quarter in 2026.
  • Anthony Elian, JPMorgan, sought clarity on Q4 expense uptick; Matthews attributed it to performance-related compensation, seasonal factors, and increased business development spending, all incorporated into the 4% NIE growth guidance.
  • Catherine Mealor, KBW, asked about loan repricing dynamics; Young provided details on both legacy and acquired loan books, indicating a net positive repricing environment and a sustainable NIM range.
  • Jonathan Rau, Barclays, queried technology investments; Young described ongoing investments in commercial loan servicing, AI, and FX platforms, with a focus on revenue-producing talent.
  • Gary Tenner, D.A. Davidson, questioned loan production in Texas and Colorado; Matthews said combined Q4 production was $888 million, up 15% from Q3, with 17 of 26 new RMs in those markets.
  • David Bishop, Hovde Group, asked about banker recruitment efforts; Corbett described an active pipeline, with conversations growing from 200 to 237 bankers quarter-over-quarter.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral-to-positive tone, focusing on sustainability of growth, margin guidance, hiring, and expense control. Questions indicated confidence in management's ability to deliver on guidance, but also probed for details on drivers and sustainability.
  • Management presented a confident and constructive outlook, using phrases like "we're carrying that momentum into 2026" and "the fundamentals of the company are as good as they've ever been." Tone in Q&A remained steady and transparent, with detailed responses and reiteration of prior guidance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts' sentiment is stable, with continued focus on growth and integration outcomes, but with increased emphasis on hiring and leveraging market disruption.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for NIM and loan growth remains consistent, with management reiterating prior targets and maintaining a stable macro outlook.
  • Strategic focus has shifted from integration risk to execution on organic growth and revenue expansion, particularly in Texas and Colorado.
  • Analysts' questions evolved from integration and credit quality concerns in Q3 to hiring plans, expense management, and the sustainability of recent performance in Q4.
  • Key metrics such as EPS, tangible book value, and payout ratios showed improvement, while expense guidance and hiring initiatives became more prominent in management's remarks.
  • Management's confidence level is unchanged, but emphasis has shifted toward growth opportunities and capital deployment, supported by strong capital ratios and operational performance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified performance-based compensation, seasonality, and the timing of hiring as drivers of higher fourth quarter expenses, but maintained that these are accounted for in 2026 guidance.
  • Questions were raised about the sustainability of noninterest income and correspondent banking performance, with management noting variability but projecting stable averages.
  • Substandard loan increases were attributed to a handful of multifamily properties in lease-up with strong equity positions, and credit concerns were downplayed.

Final Takeaway

SouthState Bank Corporation closes 2025 with integration risks largely behind it and a clear focus on organic growth, particularly in new markets. The company projects stable margins and mid- to upper single-digit loan growth for 2026 while expecting a 4% increase in noninterest expenses as it continues investing in revenue producers. Management maintains confidence in its strategic direction and capital strength, positioning the bank to leverage ongoing market disruption and drive further shareholder returns.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: enGene extends momentum with another 14% surge on Friday
data.description:
enGene Holdings Inc. (ENGN) climbed another 14% on Friday, adding to gains posted over the previous two sessions. Earlier in the week, the company announced an expanded debt facility, helping the stock extend an advance that has marked most of January so far.

Going into Friday's close, ENGN was trading at $12.12, an advance of $1.48 on the session. The rally of nearly 14% followed a similar 14% advance logged in the previous session. This followed an advance of 2.6% on Wednesday.

Overall, ENGN is on track to finish higher in eight of the past nine trading sessions. The stock has now climbed about 47% since its close on Jan. 6. Friday's surge also allowed the stock to set a new 52-week high of $12.25.

After the closing bell on Tuesday, the clinical-stage, non-viral genetic medicines company announced an expanded $125M debt facility with Hercules Capital, Inc. This included up to $100M in additional loan proceeds and $25M advanced to refinance its existing debt facility.

"This additional access to capital strengthens our balance sheet and provides us financial flexibility as we plan for a BLA filing for detalimogene in the second half of this year and potential commercial launch in 2027," CEO Ron Cooper said of the deal.

data.title: First Western signals continued NIM expansion and double-digit loan growth in 2026 amid market disruption
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: First Western Financial, Inc. (MYFW) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Scott Wylie, Founder, Chairman, CEO & President, reported "positive trends in many areas, including loan growth, net interest margin expansion, well-managed operating expenses and generally stable asset quality. This resulted in an increase in our level of profitability." He highlighted a conservative approach to loan production and described loan growth as "diversified across our markets, industries and loan types." Wylie also stated that tangible book value per share increased 1.6% in the quarter and noted a $0.10 per share after-tax reduction due to a write-down of an OREO property.
  • Julie Courkamp, COO & Director, stated loans held for investment increased $59 million from the prior quarter, with new loan production at $146 million for Q4. She emphasized, "The average rate on new production being 6.36% in this quarter."
  • Courkamp detailed deposit trends, reporting a $102 million increase in total deposits, with average deposits up 10% year-over-year. She noted outflows from title company operating accounts but highlighted successful new deposit relationships and the runoff of high-cost deposits.
  • David Weber, CFO & Treasurer, stated, "Our gross revenue increased 1.5% from the prior quarter, primarily due to an increase in net interest income. Relative to the fourth quarter of 2024, our gross revenue increased 12.2%." He added, "Our net interest income increased 5.6% from the prior quarter and 21.7% from the fourth quarter of 2024 due to an increase in our net interest margin." Weber also noted a 17 basis point improvement in NIM to 2.71%.

Outlook

  • Wylie projected, "Our loan deposit pipelines remain strong and should continue to result in solid balance sheet growth in 2026 with loan and deposit growth at similar levels to what we had in 2025." He indicated ongoing market disruption is "creating opportunities for us to add banking talent, and we will continue to take advantage of these opportunities if and when they materialize."
  • The company expects "positive trends in our net interest margin, our fee income and more operating leverage resulting from our disciplined expense control." Wylie cautioned, "We had net interest margin of 26 basis points in 2025. And while we expect further expansion in 2026, it may not be at the same level as we saw last year."
  • Management is not expecting "any meaningful deterioration in asset quality" and aims for "steady improvement in our financial performance and further value being created for our shareholders in 2026."

Financial Results

  • Reported net income was $3.3 million or $0.34 per diluted share for Q4, compared to $3.2 million or $0.32 per diluted share in Q3.
  • Noninterest income decreased by approximately $800,000 from the prior quarter due to seasonal declines in mortgage sales and lower risk management and insurance fees.
  • Operating expenses increased by $1.2 million, primarily due to a $1.4 million one-time write-down of an OREO property, while other noninterest expense categories remained consistent or decreased slightly.
  • Asset quality remained stable, with decreases in nonaccrual loans and NPAs, and the last piece of OREO is under contract for sale.

Q&A

  • Brett Rabatin, Hovde Group: Asked about NIM outlook and loan repricing. Weber responded, "We do expect further expansion to continue through 2026. Now it may not be at the same level that we saw through 2025 ... but we do expect to continue through 2026." Weber added, "We have about $250 million in fixed loans ... maturing over the next year. And those average yield on those is in the low 5s."
  • Rabatin inquired about asset/wealth management and mortgage. Wylie explained, "A lot of the lower-yielding categories and the fixed rate categories have had reductions. We're actually in the higher-yielding categories for us on the AUM side that we're seeing improvements." Courkamp reported, "Q4 mortgage production, Q1 mortgage production for us is typically lower just given the seasonality. But we continue to remain very focused as a strategic part of our business."
  • Wood Lay, KBW: Asked about expense growth and profitability targets. Wylie said, "We wanted to keep our expense below $20 million a quarter ... if we see opportunities ... we want to look at it and support you with that." On ROA, Wylie said, "We have said our near-term objective here is to get to a 1% ROA, which would be 3.50-ish."
  • Matthew Clark, Piper Sandler: Asked about deposit beta and NIM pressure. Weber stated, "Our NIM in the month of December was 2.72%." On deposit beta, Weber said, "I think we can hold that."
  • William Dezellem, Tieton Capital: Asked about mortgage loans for sale and construction/development loans. Weber explained the mortgage held-for-sale balance was "timing dynamics." Wylie said the reduction in construction loans was "much more the former than the latter"—an intentional strategy.
  • Ross Haberman, RLH Investments: Asked about mortgage market outlook and possible acquisitions. Wylie and Courkamp described ongoing investment in mortgage loan officer headcount and a focus on organic growth, not acquisitions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst questions were probing about NIM sustainability, expense discipline, asset quality, and loan growth, with a neutral to slightly positive undertone as management provided direct responses and detail on strategic initiatives.
  • Management's tone was confident, with Wylie stating, "The positive trends we're seeing in a number of key areas are expected to continue, which we believe will result in a steady improvement in our financial performance and further value being created for our shareholders in 2026." During Q&A, management remained constructive, directly addressing concerns and providing operational details.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment was more optimistic, with a stronger focus on offensive growth strategies and leveraging market disruption for talent and client acquisition.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Loan growth accelerated, with loans held for investment increasing $59 million in Q4 vs. $50 million in Q3; new loan production remained steady at $146 million both quarters.
  • Deposit growth slowed to $102 million in Q4 from $320 million in Q3, attributed to seasonal outflows, but underlying new deposit relationships remained strong.
  • Net interest margin increased 17 basis points to 2.71% in Q4 from a Q3 decline to 2.54%.
  • Operating expenses rose in Q4 due to a one-time OREO write-down, while core expenses remained flat. Noninterest income declined sequentially, reversing the Q3 increase.
  • Management's tone shifted more decisively toward growth, with a greater emphasis on market disruption opportunities and expansion into Arizona.
  • Analysts shifted from focusing on deposit stickiness and balance sheet deployment in Q3 to NIM sustainability, expense management, and asset quality in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Market competition for loans and deposits remains intense, with management emphasizing a conservative approach to underwriting and pricing.
  • Seasonal and structural declines in noninterest income raise questions about fee income growth, though management expects improvement due to leadership changes.
  • The $1.4 million OREO write-down was a one-time item, and management indicated the property is under contract for sale.
  • Construction and development lending has been intentionally reduced as a risk mitigation strategy.
  • Management noted ongoing monitoring of asset quality and the potential impact of economic shifts, but reported no signs of deterioration.

Final Takeaway

First Western Financial delivered improved profitability in the fourth quarter, driven by loan growth, disciplined expense management, and net interest margin expansion. Management expects to maintain this positive momentum into 2026, targeting continued balance sheet growth and further NIM improvement, while leveraging market disruption to attract talent and clients. The company remains focused on organic growth, maintaining asset quality, and achieving greater operating leverage, with a clear objective of reaching a 1% return on assets as key initiatives mature.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
